title: Debugging, logging and final project

canonicalExplanation.summary: Java debugging combines a reproducible failing case, breakpoints and state inspection, stack-aware hypotheses, structured diagnostics, and a regression test that proves the repair.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A conditional breakpoint stops at the first invalid balance, the learner traces its assignment backward, fixes the invariant, and adds a boundary regression test. Your goal is to deliver and explain a tested multi-package CLI application with reproducible build output. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Exceptions, resources and I/O; Lambdas, streams and optionals; JUnit tests; Resource management and serialization boundaries; Reduction and collectors. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language-specific operational model
sections.body: Java debugging combines a reproducible failing case, breakpoints and state inspection, stack-aware hypotheses, structured diagnostics, and a regression test that proves the repair. This model is applied through the declared Java: Beginner to Intermediate behavior rather than a language-neutral slogan.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure model
sections.body: A debugger can change timing, logs can leak secrets or become user-facing control flow, optimized/runtime internals may differ, and the first visible bad value can have an earlier cause. The learner must reject this documented misconception: Adding print statements until the symptom disappears is sufficient debugging evidence and production logging may include any value useful to a developer.

includes: Use debugger state and structured logging without leaking secrets or replacing error handling.
Deliver and explain a tested multi-package CLI application with reproducible build output.
excludes: A debugger can change timing, logs can leak secrets or become user-facing control flow, optimized/runtime internals may differ, and the first visible bad value can have an earlier cause.

outcomes: Deliver and explain a tested multi-package CLI application with reproducible build output.

examples.title: Debugging, logging and final project: direct language case
examples.situation: A conditional breakpoint stops at the first invalid balance, the learner traces its assignment backward, fixes the invariant, and adds a boundary regression test.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the concrete Java: Beginner to Intermediate state or tool result: A conditional breakpoint stops at the first invalid balance, the learner traces its assignment backward, fixes the invariant, and adds a boundary regression test.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.

Step 3 — Verify the result with this correction: Debug from a reproducible failure and explicit hypothesis, inspect the causal state, protect sensitive data, and preserve the fix with an automated regression test.
examples.result: The example meets the atomic outcome because Debug from a reproducible failure and explicit hypothesis, inspect the causal state, protect sensitive data, and preserve the fix with an automated regression test.

examples.title: Debugging, logging and final project: boundary and transfer case
examples.situation: Structured logging records an operation id and safe status without printing tokens, passwords, full personal data, or replacing exception propagation.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name what changed from the direct case: Structured logging records an operation id and safe status without printing tokens, passwords, full personal data, or replacing exception propagation.

Step 2 — Keep the language boundary explicit: A debugger can change timing, logs can leak secrets or become user-facing control flow, optimized/runtime internals may differ, and the first visible bad value can have an earlier cause.

Step 3 — Reject the tempting but incorrect rule: Adding print statements until the symptom disappears is sufficient debugging evidence and production logging may include any value useful to a developer.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Deliver and explain a tested multi-package CLI application with reproducible build output.

misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Predict both examples and identify the first observable contradiction in this belief: Adding print statements until the symptom disappears is sufficient debugging evidence and production logging may include any value useful to a developer.

analogy.example: a versioned delivery line whose public labels, recipe, inspections, and investigation record make one artifact reproducible can introduce Debugging, logging and final project, after the canonical Java: Beginner to Intermediate rule is stated first.
usefulFor: packages, Maven, JUnit, debugging, and project evidence
limitations: access control is not a security boundary and caches, plugins, bytecode, secrets, and platform differences need technical controls
